en: Now concerning food offered to idols: we know that "all of us possess knowledge." This "knowledge" puffs up, but love builds up.
zh: 關於祭過偶像的食物，我們曉得我們都有知識。但知識會使人自高自大，唯有愛心能造就人。

en: If anyone imagines that he knows something, he does not yet know as he ought to know.
zh: 如果有人自以為知道些甚麼，那麼，他應該知道的，他還是不知道。

en: But if anyone loves God, he is known by God.
zh: 如果有人愛 神，這人是 神所知道的。

en: Therefore, as to the eating of food offered to idols, we know that "an idol has no real existence," and that "there is no God but one."
zh: 關於吃祭過偶像的食物，我們知道世上的偶像算不得甚麼，也知道 神只有一位，沒有別的神。

en: For although there may be so-called gods in heaven or on earth- as indeed there are many "gods" and many "lords"-
zh: 雖然有被稱為神的，無論在天上或在地上 ，

en: yet for us there is one God, the Father, from whom are all things and for whom we exist, and one Lord, Jesus Christ, through whom are all things and through whom we exist.
zh: 然而我們只有一位 神，就是父；萬物都是從他而來，我們也為了他而活。我們也只有一位主，就是耶穌基督；萬物都是藉著他而有的，我們也是藉著他而有的。

en: However, not all possess this knowledge. But some, through former association with idols, eat food as really offered to an idol, and their conscience, being weak, is defiled.
zh: 不過，這種知識不是人人都有的。有些人直到現在習慣了拜偶像的事，因此他們吃的時候，就把這些食物看作是真的獻過給偶像的；他們的良心既然軟弱，就被污穢了。

en: Food will not commend us to God. We are no worse off if we do not eat, and no better off if we do.
zh: 其實食物不能使我們親近 神，我們不吃也無損，吃也無益。

en: But take care that this right of yours does not somehow become a stumbling block to the weak.
zh: 然而你們要謹慎，免得你們這自由成了軟弱的人的絆腳石。

en: For if anyone sees you who have knowledge eating in an idol's temple, will he not be encouraged, if his conscience is weak, to eat food offered to idols?
zh: 因為如果有人看見你這有知識的人，在偶像的廟裡吃喝，他的良心若是軟弱，他不就放膽去吃那祭過偶像的食物嗎？

en: And so by your knowledge this weak person is destroyed, the brother for whom Christ died.
zh: 因此，基督已經為他死了的那軟弱的弟兄，就因你的知識而滅亡了。

en: Thus, sinning against your brothers and wounding their conscience when it is weak, you sin against Christ.
zh: 你們這樣得罪弟兄，傷了他們軟弱的良心，就是得罪基督了。

en: Therefore, if food makes my brother stumble, I will never eat meat, lest I make my brother stumble.
zh: 所以，如果食物使我的弟兄跌倒，我就永遠不再吃肉，免得使我的弟兄跌倒了。
